commit.message:
refactor(specialist): migrate core prompts to yaml runtime

Refs #204

Teach the TypeScript loader to read YAML specialist definitions, make YAML override legacy Markdown for base runtime loading, and move the core English Markdown prompts into locale overlays. This makes routa, crafter, gate, and developer use YAML as their default runtime source while preserving English Markdown copies under locales/en.

commit.message:
fix(mps): eliminate MPS CPU fallbacks for `grid_sample` and bicubic backward (#821)

- `grid_sampler_2d_backward`: backward pass of `F.grid_sample` not yet
  implemented on MPS.  Replaced with `_bilinear_grid_sample` in
  `utilities/tensors.py` — gather-based index arithmetic that is native
  on every backend.  Non-MPS devices delegate to `F.grid_sample` unchanged
  (CUDA fused kernel preserved).  Affects deformable attention fallback
  (`ms_deform_attn_core_pytorch`) and segmentation point sampling
  (`point_sample`).
- `_upsample_bicubic2d_aa_backward`: anti-aliased bicubic backward not
  implemented on MPS.  Conditionally disable `antialias` on MPS in both
  DINOv2 position embedding interpolation paths (`dinov2.py`,
  `dinov2_with_windowed_attn.py`).

Adds 35 parity tests in `tests/utilities/test_tensors.py` verifying the
manual bilinear path matches `F.grid_sample` numerically (atol=1e-5) and
passes `torch.autograd.gradcheck`.

- Move padding_mode validation before shape unpack for fail-fast behaviour
- Cast bilinear weights to input.dtype to preserve float16 on MPS
- Fix mislabelled bicubic comment in dinov2.py
- Update stale point_sample docstring to reference _bilinear_grid_sample

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stt): requeue audio on socket closure

Harden the AssemblyAI streaming path so audio chunks are not lost when the socket closes during a send. Requeue failed chunks at the front of the buffer, reconnect automatically, and add regression coverage for preserving chunk order after a failed send.

feat(sip): hang up calls explicitly on local end

commit.message:
Add portable architecture-spec living docs skills (#62) closes #54 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | No repo-root `architecture-spec/` skill bundle existed, and the repo had no generated architecture inventory, Mermaid views, or living spec. | Running the new `architecture-spec` scan -> diagram -> spec -> review pipeline produces a reusable skill bundle plus committed architecture artifacts under `docs/architecture/siaan/`. | | Observable effect | Reviewers had to infer architecture directly from `elixir/lib/` and scattered docs; no drift review existed for architecture docs. | Reviewers can open `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md`, inspect five Mermaid diagrams, and read `docs/architecture/siaan/drift-review.md` for current drift evidence. | | Affected inputs | Manual code reading and ad hoc documentation were the only available inputs. | The pipeline consumes the repository tree and emits `inventory.json`, C4/UML Mermaid files, `SPEC.md`, and a drift report while preserving runtime behavior. | ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: `make -C elixir all` must stay green because the change is documentation/tooling-only. - **Explicitly unchanged**: the Elixir runtime, orchestration flow, tracker integrations, and workspace execution paths are unchanged. - **Out of scope**: parser-perfect cross-language architecture inference and fixes for the broken existing doc links found by the review pass. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Skill bundle is incomplete or not self-contained | Added `architecture-spec/scan`, `diagram`, `spec`, and `review` with local `SKILL.md`, scripts, and templates. | N/A (local diff) | | Generated documentation misses required system areas | `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md` explicitly covers `state-sync`, `workflow-engine`, `agent-bridge`, `workspace`, `dispatch`, and `orchestrator`. | N/A (generated artifact) | | Review output is noisy or fails to detect real drift | `docs/architecture/siaan/drift-review.md` reports two concrete broken repo doc references: `docs/developing.md -> ../README.md` and `elixir/README.md -> ../SPEC.md`. | N/A (generated artifact) | | Tooling/docs change regresses project health | `make -C elixir all` passed, including format, lint, coverage (`522 tests, 0 failures, 2 skipped`), and dialyzer. | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: the scan heuristics could overfit or underfit another repository and generate low-signal architecture output. - **Blast radius**: limited to the new `architecture-spec/` skill bundle and committed docs under `docs/architecture/siaan/`. - **How to detect**: reviewers can inspect the generated spec/diagrams for missing subsystem coverage or noisy drift findings; CI remains covered by `make -C elixir all`. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `86a1601`. ### Review Focus 1. Check whether the four `architecture-spec/*/SKILL.md` files actually reflect the intended Pipeline / Generator / Reviewer design patterns. 2. Check whether `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md` is enough for a human to understand siaan's architecture quickly without reading the source first. 3. Check whether the scan/review heuristics are narrow enough to keep only reviewer-usable drift findings.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) `docs/architecture/siaan/l1-system-context.mmd` models the operator, tracker, Codex app-server, and repository around the automation system boundary. ### Container (C4-L2) `docs/architecture/siaan/l2-container-view.mmd` shows the six core containers: orchestrator, dispatch, workspace, agent-bridge, workflow-engine, and state-sync. ### Component (C4-L3) `docs/architecture/siaan/l3-component-view.mmd` maps the same six components with observed dependency-strength annotations from the scan inventory. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `state-sync` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_config_store.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_source_store.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_stats.ex` - `workflow-engine` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_config.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_file.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/prompt_builder.ex` - `agent-bridge` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/agent_runner.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/app_server.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/dynamic_tool.ex` - `workspace` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/workspace.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/path_safety.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/ssh.ex` - `dispatch` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch_lifecycle.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/tracker.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github/adapter.ex` - `orchestrator` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/http_server.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/status_dashboard.ex` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: keep the implementation self-contained by embedding scripts/templates inside each skill path and commit siaan-generated artifacts as proof. - **Alternatives considered**: repo-global helper scripts, language-specific parsers, or instructions-only skills without generated validation output. - **Trade-offs**: heuristic scanning is more portable but less exact than parser-specific analysis; committing generated docs increases diff size but makes review concrete. - **Why chosen**: the issue requires a copyable root-level folder that works across repos and is immediately validated against siaan itself. - **Implementation links**: `architecture-spec/scan/scripts/scan_repo.py`, `architecture-spec/diagram/scripts/generate_diagrams.py`, `architecture-spec/spec/scripts/generate_spec.py`, `architecture-spec/review/scripts/review_drift.py`, `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md`.
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

repository.name: siaan
repository.description: Skill Is All Agents Need - GitHub Issue-driven agent orchestrator with continuous learning

commit.message:
Add Mermaid workflow-engine for orchestrator state machines (#69) closes #59 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | The repo had no `workflow-engine/` tree and no Mermaid-driven state machine implementation. | `workflow-engine/` now provides a Mermaid parser, interpreter, validator, example workflow, and skill guidance compiled into the Elixir app. | | Observable effect | Workflow logic remained embedded in tracker/orchestrator code paths, and there was no executable Mermaid configuration prototype for the GitHub issue lifecycle. | Mermaid `stateDiagram` text can now be parsed into an internal machine, executed through generic condition/action/activity callbacks, validated for structural issues, and demonstrated with a GitHub issue workflow example. | | Affected inputs | No Mermaid workflow files or engine-specific tests were recognized by Mix. | `workflow-engine/**` libraries and fixtures are compiled/tested through Mix, and repo validation uses repo-local Dialyzer PLTs so `make -C elixir all` stays reproducible in this workspace. | ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> ready ready --> in_progress: dispatch in_progress --> review: handoff review --> in_progress: rework review --> closed: merge closed --> [*] ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: The engine remains generic; it only knows about states, transitions, conditions, actions, and activities, while GitHub-specific behavior stays in the example config and surrounding orchestrator code. - **Explicitly unchanged**: Existing orchestrator/runtime behavior outside the new engine path is unchanged; the change adds an isolated engine surface plus Mix wiring and tests. - **Out of scope**: This PR does not replace the live orchestrator with the new engine yet; it creates the reusable engine, docs, and proof surface required by GH-59. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Mermaid parsing loses workflow metadata or edge semantics | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` covers notes, annotation-only labels, action lists, invalid diagrams, and the GitHub issue example | N/A (local command) | | Execution or graph analysis semantics regress repo validation gates | `make -C elixir all` passed, including build, format, lint, 534 tests with 100% coverage, and Dialyzer using repo-local PLTs | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Future callers could rely on Mermaid conventions the parser does not yet support. - **Blast radius**: `workflow-engine/**`, Mix compile/format/dialyzer config, and the new workflow-engine ExUnit coverage file. - **How to detect**: `mix test test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` or `make -C elixir all` will fail on parser/interpreter/validator regressions. - **How to rollback**: Revert commit `94cbca4` to remove the engine tree and its Mix wiring. ### Review Focus 1. Verify the Mermaid extension conventions are minimal but sufficient for conditions, actions, and state activities. 2. Check that the interpreter/validator APIs stay generic and do not leak GitHub-specific assumptions. 3. Confirm the Mix wiring changes are the right long-term approach for keeping `workflow-engine/` self-contained at repo root.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change — this is an internal orchestrator/workflow-engine capability addition with no new external system boundary. ### Container (C4-L2) No new container — the existing Elixir application now compiles and validates repo-root workflow-engine libraries alongside its current modules. ### Component (C4-L3) - `workflow-engine/mermaid-parser` parses Mermaid `stateDiagram` text into `SymphonyElixir.WorkflowEngine.StateMachine`. - `workflow-engine/interpreter` evaluates conditions and executes actions/activities against the parsed machine. - `workflow-engine/validate` analyzes the machine for unreachable states, deadlocks, and missing conditions. - `workflow-engine/test` provides shared examples used by `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs`. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `workflow-engine/mermaid-parser/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/mermaid_parser.ex` - `workflow-engine/interpreter/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/interpreter.ex` - `workflow-engine/validate/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/validator.ex` - `workflow-engine/examples/github_issue_workflow.mmd` - `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` - `elixir/mix.exs` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Keep the new engine self-contained under repo-root `workflow-engine/` while compiling it into the Elixir app through explicit Mix path wiring. - **Alternatives considered**: Move the modules into `elixir/lib`, or leave the engine as documentation-only scaffolding without executable parser/interpreter/validator code. - **Trade-offs**: The repo-root layout matches the issue structure and keeps the engine conceptually isolated, but it requires explicit Mix formatter/compiler/dialyzer configuration. - **Why chosen**: GH-59 specifically calls for a self-contained `workflow-engine/` folder, and executable proof was required immediately rather than a docs-only placeholder. - **Implementation links**: `workflow-engine/**`, `elixir/mix.exs`, `elixir/.formatter.exs`, `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
replace TargSubsRaw with DialogueView to fix STT/translit regression

Commit 206e3bd introduced TargSubsRaw to preserve non-Default ASS styles during
transliteration, but this created a deep-copy snapshot that never received STT
updates from processDubtitles. When dubtitles and transliteration were both
enabled, romanized output was based on the original subtitle text rather than
the STT/ASR transcript the user had requested.

The root cause was maintaining two diverging copies of the subtitle structure.
This replaces the two-copy model with a single canonical TargSubs (never
filtered in-place) plus a DialogueView that provides indexed access to Default-
style dialogue items only. Since view items are pointers into the canonical
structure, STT mutations through the view propagate automatically to all
consumers, including transliteration.

∙ add DialogueView type to subs package with NewDialogueView, Item, Len,
  RawIndex methods
∙ add IsDefaultStyle as exported function in subs package
∙ add Subs2DubsFromView that operates through the view
∙ remove TargSubsRaw from Task struct
∙ prepareSubtitles creates DialogueView instead of deep-copy + in-place filter
∙ Supervisor, ProcessItem, and all item counting go through DialogueView
∙ GetCompleteSubtitleText and CreateSubtitlesFromText take DialogueView for
  index mapping
∙ summary and condensed-audio paths use dialogue-only items

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: routa
repository.description: Build Your Agent Team for Real-World AI Development - Workspace-first multi-agent coordination platform for AI development, with shared Specs, Kanban orchestration, and MCP/ACP/   A2A support across web and desktop.

commit.message:
feat(agents): implement Phase 4 Agents (#11)

* feat(cli): implement Phase 3 CLI commands

- Add Commander.js entry point with all subcommands
- Add init command: create config file and sync labels
- Add status command: show repo and issue/PR summary
- Add issue command: create and list issues
- Add pr command: create and list pull requests
- Add review command: transition issue state and find related PRs
- Add auto command: run automation cycle
- Add shared utilities for command context
- Add @inquirer/prompts for interactive prompts
- Add listIssues and listPullRequests to GitHub modules
- Add getCurrentBranch and isWorkingTreeClean to git utils
- Add comprehensive tests (42 tests total)

Closes #4

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* fix(cli): improve CLI consistency and validation

- Add --json option to init command for output consistency
- Add --issue validation (reject NaN, 0, negative numbers)
- Fix related PR matching with word boundary regex
- Add ValidationError type and handler
- Update README with CLI command usage examples
- Add CLI entry point test

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* fix(cli): tighten review --issue validation and fix README

- Use regex to strictly validate integer-only issue numbers
- Reject 1.5, 12abc etc. as invalid issue numbers
- Update README: "共通オプション" → "各サブコマンド共通オプション"
- Add tests for invalid issue number cases

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* feat(agents): implement Phase 4 Agents

Implement 6 agents for autonomous development:
- BaseAgent: Common interface and runtime context resolution
- IssueAgent: Type detection (bug/feature) and needs-doc evaluation
- CodeGenAgent: Source code generation only (no docs)
- DocAgent: Documentation generation (conditional: needs-doc/@doc/auto)
- ReviewAgent: ESLint + TypeScript checks with score calculation
- PRAgent: PR creation with Closes #N and merge commit support
- CoordinatorAgent: State machine orchestration

Closes #5

Co-Authored-By: Codex X <codex@openai.com>

* fix(agents): improve ReviewAgent and CoordinatorAgent reliability

- ReviewAgent: Auto-skip ESLint when not configured (prevents false failures)
- CoordinatorAgent: Transition to 'blocked' on PR failure instead of 'done'
- Add @vitest/coverage-v8 for coverage reporting
- Add regression tests for both fixes

Co-Authored-By: Codex X <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: MizukiMachine <negichanfunakun@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill simulator eeprom fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased simulator EEPROM fix placeholder with commit
  35ebde7

Rationale:
- the changelog should reference the shipped fix commit rather than a
  pending marker

Validation:
- rg -n "pending hash|\\(pending hash\\)" CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(simulator): zero-pad out-of-range EEPROM window reads

Summary:
- make ESCImage chunk out-of-range EEPROM windows return zero-padded
  data instead of calling binary_part/3 with an invalid offset
- add regression coverage for EEPROM reads beyond the end of the
  generated image and keep the release note in the changelog

Rationale:
- raw simulator startup could crash when the EEPROM window address moved
  beyond the image bounds, which blocked the healthy raw transport
  matrix path even though the correct behavior is to return zero-filled
  data

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator/slave/esc_image_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(tests): avoid fluent CI race in languages test (#15)

Summary:
- switch the fluent `languages()` test from `load_no_iso` to `load`
- document why the test must avoid toggling global isolating state

Rationale:
- archived CI logs show the first failure was
  `test_isolating_disabled_no_marks` receiving isolation marks
- `load_no_iso` mutates the global `use_isolating` flag, so calling it from a
  non-serialized test can race with the rest of the fluent suite and poison
  the shared test mutex

Tests:
- for i in $(seq 1 50); do cargo test -q -p egui-i18n --features fluent --test fluent || exit 1; done
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n --features classic -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p egui-i18n --features classic --test classic
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n --features fluent -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p egui-i18n --features fluent --test fluent
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-cli -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-cli
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-example-classic -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-example-classic
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-example-fluent -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-example-fluent

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Restore GitHub issue sync behavior

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill runtime freshness and transport hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased pending hash entries for the runtime
  freshness, simulator transport, and simulator README updates with
  commit 11d1af2

Rationale:
- the changelog should point at the shipped change commit instead of
  carrying placeholder markers

Validation:
- rg -n "pending hash|\\(pending hash\\)" CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add PDO freshness and transport fault split

Summary:
- make input reads fail closed on stale cached PDO data and expose
  explicit domain/slave freshness diagnostics derived from valid LRW
  refresh timing
- move simulator transport ownership under
  EtherCAT.Simulator.Transport.*, add mode-aware raw transport fault
  control, and align simulator docs/tests with the new boundary
- remove tracked todo planning notes now that todo/ is ignored and
  should stay out of the repo

Rationale:
- control-path reads should surface stale process data explicitly
  instead of silently handing back old cached inputs
- simulator transport concerns were previously split across core,
  UDP, and raw endpoint modules; the new namespace makes ownership
  clearer without forcing fake symmetry between transports
- keeping todo/ untracked reduces repo noise and avoids shipping local
  planning artifacts

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix docs --warnings-as-errors --formatter html
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/integration/support/expect_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/04_corrupted_frame_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/38_redundant_passthrough_only_partial_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/40_redundant_partial_processed_reply_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(parity): align is-rs behavior with is.js (#14)

Summary:
- tighten arithmetic, string, presence, time, and type predicates to
  better match is.js semantics
- add generic presence helpers to the object-style API and expose
  null/undefined/same_type type checks
- document the implementation approach and parity decisions in the
  README and a new technical overview

Rationale:
- the crate already covered much of is.js, but several predicates still
  diverged in edge-case behavior or were missing from the facade
- documenting the architecture and parity trade-offs makes the Rust
  port easier to extend without reintroducing semantic drift

Tests:
- cargo fmt && cargo test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Signed-off-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
